scene.scenePrompt: Persian miniature painting style, vertical composition, flattened perspective, intricate gold filigree borders. A luxurious palace garden in warm afternoon light, dappled shadows cast by tall cypress trees. A magnificent sand-inlaid game board, embedded with rubies, sits at the center of a tiled courtyard, a half-finished chess game with mechanical automatons on its surface. Fatima stands at the edge of the cypress shadow, holding a gold-stamped parchment scroll, a subtle smile on her lips. The atmosphere is serene, suffused with golden-hour warmth, decorative jeweled colors, and a sense of quiet revelation.

beats.narration: Your slender fingers slide the gold-thread puppet forward. As the piece lands, the rubies on the sand table glow faintly. From the distant palace corridor comes the hearty applause of Sultan Azhar—you have won again.

activeCharacters.name: Fatima
activeCharacters.pose: Standing at the edge of the cypress shadows, holding a roll of gold-embossed parchment, with a faint, elusive smile playing on her lips

beats.narration: You do not look up. At the edge of the chessboard, a page of the fragmented manual suddenly glows with a faint golden light. A line of text that has never appeared before emerges from the depths of the paper like a living thing.
beats.line: The Library, third shelf, the Book of Death.
beats.lineDelivery: (Words appearing on the manual) A whisper as the ancient script emerges, carrying the gritty texture of grinding stones

activeCharacters.name: Fatima
activeCharacters.pose: Noticing you staring at the manual, her smile deepens as she slowly approaches

beats.speaker: Fatima
beats.line: My lord scholar, you seem far more interested in that fragmented manual than in the Sultan's praise.
beats.lineDelivery: Her voice is gentle, but carries a hint of perceptive amusement, spoken at a measured, unhurried pace

activeCharacters.name: Fatima
activeCharacters.pose: Standing across the chessboard, gently placing the gold-embossed parchment on the edge of the sand table

beats.narration: You close the manual, feeling the residual warmth of the pages on your fingertips. The 'new manual' Fatima brought smells of the library's musty parchment and ink, but the wax seal on it bears the Sultan's personal crest—a cobra clutching an hourglass.
beats.speaker: You
beats.line: Lady Fatima is always so... timely.

activeCharacters.name: Fatima
activeCharacters.pose: With her hands clasped in front of her, she meets your gaze with an open, steady look

choices.label: Ask directly about the 'Book of Death'
Take the new manual, remaining expressionless
Question her about the note instead

beats.speaker: You
beats.line: The Book of Death—the third shelf of the library. Lady Fatima, could you show me the way?

activeCharacters.name: Fatima
activeCharacters.pose: A flash of surprise crosses her eyes for a split second before she regains her gentle composure

beats.narration: You reach out to take the parchment scroll. As you exchange it, Fatima's fingertips brush against the back of your hand, seemingly by accident, feeling slightly cool.
beats.speaker: Fatima
beats.line: The Sultan said your moves just now reminded him of an old acquaintance. This manual might take you further... or into greater danger.
beats.lineDelivery: Her voice drops and her pace quickens, the last few words almost dissolving into the wind

activeCharacters.name: Fatima
activeCharacters.pose: Her fingertips lightly touch the back of your hand, but her gaze drifts toward the distant palace minarets

beats.speaker: You
beats.line: The warning on the previous note... was it your doing, or the move of another player?

activeCharacters.name: Fatima
activeCharacters.pose: Her smile vanishes completely, her eyes turning deep and complex

beats.speaker: Fatima
beats.line: In this palace, my lord scholar, the word 'player'... can be a death warrant in itself.
beats.lineDelivery: Her voice is extremely soft, yet every word is crystal clear, carrying a subtle warning

activeCharacters.name: Fatima
activeCharacters.pose: Leaning forward slightly, she lowers her voice, her eyes scanning the surroundings

characters.name: Fatima
characters.voiceDescription: Female, early 20s. Her voice is clear and warm like a fresh spring, with a calm and intellectual tone. Her speaking pace is moderately slow with crisp pronunciation. She speaks with a standard Persian accent, carrying a scholar's serenity and a palace prisoner's forbearance. Her emotions are restrained, but she reveals firm resolve at critical moments.
characters.visualDescription: Young Persian scholar, early 20s, dark hair tied back in a low ponytail, warm brown eyes with a thoughtful and observant gaze, delicate facial features with a soft jawline, wearing ornate deep crimson and gold embroidered robes with intricate floral patterns, a subtle jeweled clasp at the collar, overall elegant and scholarly demeanor with a hint of hidden resolve

storyState.logline: An imprisoned heretical scholar manipulates minds and stirs up court intrigue on the Sultan's chessboard using a fragmented manual and gold-thread puppets, all to win his only chance at reaching the ruins of the old gods beneath the desert.
storyState.genreTags: Political Intrigue / Palace Conspiracy / Mysterious Ruins / Fast-Paced Twists / First-Person Immersion
storyState.protagonist: You are an heretical scholar imprisoned deep within the Sultan's palace. Currently, you are forced to play a deadly game: each victory grants you a clue, while any defeat or exposure of your true intentions could cost you your life. You desperately seek to uncover the secrets of the desert's old god ruins using the fragmented manual and gold-thread puppets to ensure your survival or exact revenge. Your weakness is that the manual is missing a crucial page, and deep down, you have begun to place an unwarranted trust in someone within this gilded cage who appears to be an ally.
storyState.castNotes:
Sultan Azhar: A young and suspicious ruler, the creator of the chess game. On the surface, he admires your skill, but in reality, he views you as a tool to explore the ruins; his favor and his death warrant are but a thought apart.
Fatima: The clever female official in charge of the Royal Library, and the only one who knows some of the manual's secrets. She approaches you under the guise of academic exchange, but her eyes hide calculations deeper than the chessboard itself; you cannot tell if she is an ally or another player.
Old Servant Qasim: A taciturn palace elder who has served three generations of Sultans. He seems to take extra care of you, occasionally 'accidentally' revealing palace secrets, but where do his true loyalties lie?
storyState.synopsis: You win the third game of chess in the palace garden, and the fragmented page triggers the clue to the 'Book of Death'. Fatima arrives with the Sultan's new manual, leaving you with a choice: ask about the clue directly, study the new manual first, or question her true stance.
openThreads: The 'Book of Death' clue from the fragmented page points to the third shelf of the library.
What is hidden in the new manual brought by Fatima? Why did the Sultan send it now?
Fatima's true stance—is she an ally, or another player?
The connection between the missing final page of the manual and the ruins of the old gods in the desert.
relationships: Sultan Azhar: Appears appreciative on the surface, but actually views you as a tool to explore the ruins; he has grown suspicious of your moves.
Fatima: Approaches you under the cover of academic pursuit, her eyes unfathomable; she seems to know more secrets, but her motives remain unclear.
Old Servant Qasim: Has not appeared yet, but his 'care' might be a form of surveillance.
storyState.nextHook: Next Scene: Searching for the 'Book of Death' in the shadows of the library's third shelf, or uncovering the secrets of the new manual. Fatima's next move will be crucial.

worldSetting:
This is an AI interactive story game targeted at a 【Male-oriented】 audience, featuring intense dramatic conflicts and rapid twists in the style of short-form viral videos.
Story Style: Multi-line twists. Pacing: Tight and exhilarating.
Opening setting of the featured story 'The Persian Gambit': An heretical scholar imprisoned in the Sultan's palace manipulates gold-thread puppets on a chessboard using a fragmented ancient manual, stirring up court intrigue. With every game he wins, he takes one step closer to uncovering the ruins of the old gods sleeping beneath the desert.
Please dive straight into this opening to give the player a strong sense of immersion and instant gratification; subsequent branches should maintain the twist density of short dramas, ensuring players see immediate consequences for every choice.
styleGuide: Persian miniature style, vertical composition. An attractive young Persian scholar in ornate robes, sitting in a beautiful tiled garden under a cypress tree, playing a gold-plated chess set with mechanical automatons. Flattened perspective, intricate gold filigree, jeweled colors, decorative borders.
